children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish in the Institute the National Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases Advisory Board (here­after in this section referred to as the “Advisory Board”).

children.label: A
children.text: twelve members from individuals who are scientists, physicians, and other health professionals, who are not officers or employees of the United States, and who represent the specialties and disciplines relevant to arthritis, musculoskeletal diseases, and skin diseases; and

children.label: B
children.text: eight members from the general public who are knowledgeable with respect to such diseases, including one member who is a person who has such a disease, one person who is the parent of an adult with such a disease, and two members who are parents of children with arthritis.

children.label: A
children.text: the Assistant Secretary for Health, the Director of NIH, the Director of the National Institute of Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases, the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the Under Secretary for Health of the Department of Veterans Affairs, and the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Health Affairs (or the designees of such officers), and

children.label: B
children.text: such other officers and employees of the United States as the Secretary determines necessary for the Advisory Board to carry out its functions.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: c Compensation
children.text: Members of the Advisory Board who are officers or employees of the Federal Government shall serve as members of the Advisory Board without compensation in addition to that received in their regular public employment. Other members of the Advisory Board shall receive compensation at rates not to exceed the daily equivalent of the annual rate in effect for grade GS–18 of the General Schedule for each day (including traveltime) they are engaged in the performance of their duties as members of the Advisory Board.

children.heading: Term of office; vacancy
children.label: d Term of office; vacancy
children.text: The term of office of an appointed member of the Advisory Board is four years. Any member appointed to fill a vacancy for an unexpired term shall be appointed for the remainder of such term. A member may serve after the expiration of the member’s term until a successor has taken office. If a vacancy occurs in the Advisory Board, the Secretary shall make an appointment to fill the vacancy not later than 90 days after the date the vacancy occurred.

children.heading: Chairman
children.label: e Chairman
children.text: The members of the Advisory Board shall select a chairman from among the appointed members.

children.heading: Executive director, professional and clerical staff; administrative support services and facilities
children.label: f Executive director, professional and clerical staff; administrative support services and facilities
children.text: The Secretary shall, after consultation with and consideration of the recommendations of the Advisory Board, provide the Advisory Board with an executive director and one other professional staff member. In addition, the Secretary shall, after consultation with and consideration of the recommendations of the Advisory Board, provide the Advisory Board with such additional professional staff members, such clerical staff members, and (through contracts or other arrangements) with such administrative support services and facilities, such information, and such services of consultants, as the Secretary determines are necessary for the Advisory Board to carry out its functions.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: g Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Board shall meet at the call of the chairman or upon request of the Director of the Institute, but not less often than four times a year.
review and evaluate the implementation of the plan prepared under and periodically update the plan to ensure its continuing relevance;section 285d–1(a) of this title

for the purpose of assuring the most effective use and organization of resources respecting arthritis, musculoskeletal diseases and skin diseases, advise and make recommendations to the Congress, the Secretary, the Director of NIH, the Director of the Institute, and the heads of other appropriate Federal agencies for the implementation and revision of such plan; and

maintain liaison with other advisory bodies for Federal agencies involved in the implementation of such plan, the interagency coordinating committees for such diseases established under , and with key non-Federal entities involved in activities affecting the control of such dis­eases.section 285d–4 of this title

children.heading: Subcommittees; establishment and membership
children.label: i Subcommittees; establishment and membership
children.text: In carrying out its functions, the Advisory Board may establish subcommittees, convene workshops and conferences, and collect data. Such subcommittees may be composed of Advisory Board members and nonmember consultants with expertise in the particular area addressed by such subcommittees. The subcommittees may hold such meetings as are necessary to enable them to carry out their activities.

children.heading: Termination of predecessor board; time within which to appoint members
children.label: j Termination of predecessor board; time within which to appoint members
children.text: The National Arthritis Advisory Board in existence on , shall terminate upon the appointment of a successor Board under subsection (a). The Secretary shall make appointments to the Advisory Board established under subsection (a) before the expiration of 90 days after . The member of the Board in existence on , may be appointed, in accordance with subsections (b) and (d), to the Advisory Board established under subsection (a).November 20, 1985November 20, 1985November 20, 1985
